     The Numbers Business Report

Our Idiot Brother (2011)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $24,814,830Details
International Box Office $1,046,419
Worldwide Box Office $25,861,249
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $8,410,553
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,125,542
Total $9,536,095
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Every family has one: the sibling who is always just a little bit behind the curve when it comes to getting his life together. For sisters Liz, Miranda and Natalie, that person is their perennially upbeat brother Ned, an erstwhile organic farmer whose willingness to rely on the honesty of mankind is a less-than-optimum strategy for a tidy, trouble-free existence. Ned may be utterly lacking in common sense, but he is their brother and so, after his girlfriend dumps him and boots him off the farm, his sisters once again come to his rescue. As Liz, Emily and Natalie each take a turn at housing Ned, their brother’s unfailing commitment to honesty creates more than a few messes in their comfortable routines. But as each of their lives begins to unravel, Ned’s family comes to realize that maybe, in believing and trusting the people around him, Ned isn’t such an idiot after all.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$7,011,631 (28.3% of total gross)
Legs:3.54 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:96.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$5,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,555 opening theaters/2,555 max. theaters, 3.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $37,425,651

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 26th, 2011 (Wide) by Weinstein Co.
International Releases: May 17th, 2012 (Wide) (Austria)
May 17th, 2012 (Wide) (Germany)
May 23rd, 2012 (Wide) (Belgium)
May 31st, 2012 (Wide) (Netherlands)
July 4th, 2012 (Wide) (Italy)
Video Release: November 29th, 2011 by Weinstein Co./Anchor Bay
MPA Rating: R for sexual content including nudity, and for language throughout
(Rating bulletin 2178, 6/29/2011)
Running Time: 95 minutes
Plot point: Delayed Adulthood, Documentarian, Dysfunctional Family, Infidelity, LGBTQ+, Life on the Outside, Narcotics, Relationship Advice, Relationships Gone Wrong
Social setting: Ballet
Subgenre: Comedy Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Big Beach, Likely Story
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Every family has one: the sibling who is always just a little bit behind the curve when it comes to getting his life together. For sisters Liz, Miranda and Natalie, that person is their perennially upbeat brother Ned, an erstwhile organic farmer whose willingness to rely on the honesty of mankind is a less-than-optimum strategy for a tidy, trouble-free existence. Ned may be utterly lacking in common sense, but he is their brother and so, after his girlfriend dumps him and boots him off the farm, his sisters once again come to his rescue. As Liz, Emily and Natalie each take a turn at housing Ned, their brother’s unfailing commitment to honesty creates more than a few messes in their comfortable routines. But as each of their lives begins to unravel, Ned’s family comes to realize that maybe, in believing and trusting the people around him, Ned isn’t such an idiot after all.
